# How decisive were these results?

Seven face-offs this week, which means seven chances to see who has actual opinions and who's basically flipping a coin.


::: {.cell}

:::


**TL;DR:** Pickles was the most one-sided fight in the building — Dill demolished Bread & Butter 34–9 (79%), just barely out-landsliding Pizza Style's New York (35–10). The most genuine toss-up was Orange Juice: Pulp over No Pulp, 27–22 (p = 0.57) — statistically indistinguishable from a coin flip.


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}

![Leader shares with 95% confidence intervals, ranked from most lopsided to closest.](index_files/figure-html/fig-leader-ci-1.png){#fig-leader-ci width=768}

# Do New York pizza people also love a dill pickle?

Both are tangy, no-nonsense, East Coast-coded choices. Let's see how much the crowds could overlap.


::: {.cell}

:::


Of the 43 overlapping voters, somewhere between 24 and 34 (56%–79%) are Team New York *and* Team Dill, versus about 26 (61%) expected if the two tastes were unrelated. Both options win so convincingly on their own that the "tangy and no-nonsense" coalition is large no matter how the overlap actually shakes out.
